original_abstract:
In this study, two pyrazole derivatives—2-(5-methyl-1H-pyrazole-3-carbonyl)-N-phenylhydrazine-1-carboxamide (Pyz-1) and 4-amino-5-(5-methyl-1H-pyrazol-3-yl)-4H-1,2,4-triazole-3-thiol (Pyz-2)—were synthesized and characterized using 1H-NMR, 13C-NMR, FT-IR, and mass spectrometry. To understand their fundamental characteristics, we performed a comprehensive optimization of their molecular structures and predicted their electronic and thermodynamic properties in both gas and aqueous phases using the hybrid B3LYP method with 6-311++G** basis sets. The biological potential of Pyz-1 and Pyz-2 was evaluated through in vitro assays targeting anti-diabetic, antioxidant, and xanthine oxidase inhibitory activities. 

Regarding anti-diabetic performance, both compounds demonstrated potent inhibition of α-glucosidase and α-amylase. Specifically, Pyz-1 and Pyz-2 yielded IC50 values of 75.62 ± 0.56 µM and 95.85 ± 0.92 µM for α-glucosidase, and 119.3 ± 0.75 µM and 120.2 ± 0.68 µM for α-amylase, respectively, results that are highly comparable to the standard drug Acarbose. In xanthine oxidase assays, the compounds exhibited remarkable inhibitory strength, with IC50 values of 24.32 ± 0.78 µM (Pyz-1) and 10.75 ± 0.54 µM (Pyz-2). Furthermore, the synthesized derivatives showed significant antioxidant and radical scavenging capabilities. These experimental findings were complemented by molecular docking simulations, which successfully elucidated the binding modes and affinity energies between the compounds and the active sites of the α-glucosidase and α-amylase enzymes.
humanized_abstract:
Two pyrazole derivatives viz., 2-(5-methyl-1H-pyrazole-3-carbonyl)-N-phenylhydrazine-1-carboxamide (Pyz-1) and 4-amino-5-(5-methyl-1H-pyrazol-3-yl)-4H-1,2,4-triazole-3-thiol (Pyz-2) were synthesized and their spectral data like 1H-NMR, 13C-NMR, FT-IR and mass spectra were determined. Density functional theory (DFT) approach with hybrid B3LYP method was employed to optimize the geometries of the derivatives and to evaluate the electronic and thermodynamic parameters such as bond order, Mulliken charges, dipole moment, atomic population analysis, electronegativities, van der Waals radius, HOMO and LUMO energies, energy gap, ENM, ΔHm, ΔSmo, ΔUo, ΔGo, ΔSo, ETP and Vesp in gas as well as aqueous phases using 6-311++G** basis sets. Anti-diabetic, antioxidant and xanthine oxidase inhibitory activities of Pyz-1 and Pyz-2 have been investigated by performing in vitro experiments.

In order to evaluate the anti-diabetic effect of the prepared compounds and to identify their potential interaction with enzymes, in vitro studies were carried out that include inhibition of α-glucosidase, α-amylase enzymes and xanthine oxidase. Therefore, Pyz-1 and Pyz-2 were tested for inhibition of α-glucosidase, α-amylase activities using Acarbose as a standard drug. From the inhibition experiments, it was found that IC50 values of Pyz-1 and Pyz-2 were 75.62 ± 0.56 µM and 95.85 ± 0.92 µM for α-glucosidase and 119.3 ± 0.75 µM and 120.2 ± 0.68 µM for α-amylase respectively which are highly comparable to the standard Acarbose. Xanthine oxidase inhibition is very crucial due to their role in hypertension and also linked with diabetes mellitus. Hence, the in vitro xanthine oxidase inhibition studies revealed strong inhibitory activity towards xanthine oxidase having IC50% inhibitory concentration (IC50) values of 24.32 ± 0.78 µM and 10.75 ± 0.54 µM for Pyz-1 and Pyz-2 respectively. The antioxidant, free radical scavenging activities of the synthesized derivatives were investigated through DPPH, ABTS method and their corresponding EC50 values were determined. A positive correlation between antioxidant potency and the number of fluorine atoms present at the pyrazole ring for all the compounds was revealed. To elucidate more on the biological behavior of the compounds towards the selected enzymes, computational modeling approach namely molecular docking studies of compounds with α-glucosidase and α-amylase enzymes identified the binding modes and affinities towards their active sites.

document.output:

In this study, two pyrazole derivatives—2-(5-methyl-1H-pyrazole-3-carbonyl)-N-phenylhydrazine-1-carboxamide (Pyz-1) and 4-amino-5-(5-methyl-1H-pyrazol-3-yl)-4H-1,2,4-triazole-3-thiol (Pyz-2)—were synthesized and characterized using 1H-NMR, 13C-NMR, FT-IR, and mass spectrometry. Specifically, Pyz-1 and Pyz-2 yielded IC50 values of 75.62 ± 0.56 µM and 95.85 ± 0.92 µM for α-glucosidase, and 119.3 ± 0.75 µM and 120.2 ± 0.68 µM for α-amylase, respectively, results that are highly comparable to the standard drug Acarbose.
